Overall experience with Lazada has been pleasant thus far. Everything functions as expected.FeaturesIncentives such as coins (when you review items) can help you get items for cheaper, even more so when it’s on a special sale.There’s an ‘Ask the Buyers’ feature that comes in handy when needing more information before purchasing a product - past buyers will be able to answer those questions. It’s a nice community thing that I’m all for.Lazada CHOICE lets you get discounted add-ons (upsell) when you buy x amount (I’ve thus far encountered Buy 5 Get X) Website experienceFair. It could be a bit hard to search for items compared to the app but everything else was alright. UI isn’t cluttered by any means and it sticks to a simple grid system, but it does look messy and overwhelming at times when the rows are stacked a little too close together, a little more breathing space would be nice.App experiencePop ups can get annoying especially when you open the app for the first time in the day. You’re invited to play games (Lazland), unlock exclusive vouchers by cracking eggs (LuckyEgg), spinning wheels for chances to win things… it can get pretty overwhelming. Tabs are easy to navigate: Home, Fashion (Taobao Fashion), Messages, Cart and Account.For Taobao Fashion and they’d have Buy X for -X% off and Free Shipping.Via popups, it’s easier to collect daily and weekly vouchers compared to the website.ReviewsReview quality have taken a bit of a downward turn when Lazada started providing phrases and words — which I believe it was meant to help reviewers if they’re at a loss for words (e.g. dumb as rocks and can’t string a few sentences together) But now people could just spam these phrases and be done with it. So it does get a bit hard to make a purchase from a vendor when you’ve no quality reviews to go by.GamesWhen you’re passing the time and have nothing better to do, some games aren’t too bad.MergeBoss, GogoMatch, LazFun, Crack & Win, CapyBistro and StyleStar. I’ve only tried MergeBoss - addictive and GogoMatch - you’re able to earn some LazCash which shaves off cents off your purchases. Product catalog Pretty vast when all things considered, though it may not compare to Shopee in terms of product catalog scale as Shopee has more vendors with more options but I still find most of what I need on Lazada. Certain vendors such as indie shops and artists have a higher likelihood of being on Shopee than Lazada. Cancellations, returns and refundsI’ve only made cancellations and requested for returns or refunds within reason. With sufficient photo proof and acting promptly (+ being sure to return products in the state which it came in), I haven’t had issues. Lazada has allowed me to return items and refunds have been relatively prompt. Tracking and shipping servicesAll good at the time of this review. Parcel tracking is constantly updated and couriers would send me a message when my parcel has been delivered.

I had been using the platform without problem for several years. Items ordered, order gets delivered and I mark as delivered after checking the items, and platform will pay the seller. If I do not mark the item as delivered, it will be automatically marked as delivered and the money will be released to the seller after a certain period of time. There has been several times items are out of stock and the sellers would inform me to file the refund upon receiving and I got my money back without a problem. My recent problem occurred when the seller sent an incomplete parcel, where I then filed a refund upon receiving. The seller told me to cancel the refund claiming that they’ve sent a replacement for the missing item. Trusting the seller, I cancelled the refund request and later found out from the seller who blamed the logistics for losing the item and there’s actually no replacement being sent out after I asked for the tracking information of the replacement item.  I tried again to raise a refund and was denied by the platform even after going through customer service. (Twice)Hence, with the platform AND the seller holding no accountability for this issue, I practically paid for something I did not receive and there’s no way of getting the money refunded. Update on 29/3/2025: Seller did made a reshipment from their warehouse and that item arrived this morning. I did receive the purchased item eventually. However, my experience in the refund process made me realise the amount problematic aspects in this e-commerce platform. The platform is not impartial and showed to be protecting the sellers more than the customers. If the seller refused to refund the money, the platform will protect them instead of customer's wish for a refund. Hence, purchase with caution on this platform.

Told to wait and new one will be sent in 2-3working days. 4Nov spoke to Eliza. 12Nov spoke to Rose since new returm airwaybill still not received. Emphasised the last date of return in the app is 13Nov. Rose managed to get the airwaybill sent within the day as promised but when we tried to use the airwaybill again did not work! Finally spoke to Sita 13Nov and still no resolution. Even refund is not allowed, cannot speak to manager, unable to switch from drop off at courier to pickup from site. Frustrating trying to wait for customer service to respond, waste of time and petrol travelling. Very disappointed as long time customer. Will be switching to competitor platform for purchases.

For all those who have been scammed by Lazada’s sellers complain to the FTEB, address and                             below. Their email address is on their webpage. My message to Lazada follows: Please advise your CEO that your company has been reported to the Fair Trade Enforcement Bureau (FTEB), as it is about time Lazada Philippines did something about the high number of scam sellers, advertising branded products but delivering counterfeit and fake items, you have your system. You have on your database hundreds of products for sale, at prices, it is obvious they are scams so why are you allowing this and why are you not doing something about it? So many items are being advertised that are so cheap they cannot be real. Mobile Phones for under a P100 are just one of the many examples. I for one, (and this is my formal complaint to the FTEB) purchased an OPPO phone for just under P1700 and that turned out to be a fake. To get my money back, I have to wait 5 days for the seller to agree, and if he does not then a further wait until the seller agrees to the refund, and if he does not I have to wait a further period for Lazada to investigate.  Even then the refund gets put into my wallet, not a cash refund to my bank account. Disgraceful as this is, in my opinion, putting into the wallet, is getting the customer to use the money to make further purchases rather than giving the option to send it immediately to the purchaser’s bank account.LAZADA should carry out checks to assess their sellers' honesty and integrity. I have identified many so I think by looking at buyer’s reviews and complaints, they could easily identify and hopefully block them. I have asked the DTIs (PH) Fair Trade Enforcement Bureau (FTEB), being the proper agency that has jurisdiction to investigate and can best handle my concern. I am also recommending anyone who has similar problems do the same to make Lazada do something about all the fake products being advertised as other genuine branded products. I will publish their details in every media outlet so people are pre-warned. Their email and address is as follows1-3F UPRC Bldg.,315 Sen. Gil J. Puyat Ave., Makati CityTelefax: (+632) 7215.1165Cellphone: (0917) 137.3796

lazada calls themselves the 'amazon of thailand'.  well, it is partially true as they have an enormous amount of products.big difference though.  Amazon is very 'buyer' oriented whereas lazada is very 'seller' oriented.  if you want to return something on Amazon, it's NO problem at all to do.  whereas with lazada, you want to return something and lazada (who gives 7 day return policy) then goes to seller to ask if product can be returned.in my case, i bought qty 3 of a product from lazada.  of the three products, two were defective.  i sent a return request and to my surprise and disappointment lazada said they would ask  the seller if ok.  the seller rejected my return request.  so i am stuck with faulty product.  nos that is LOUSY customer service.  so BEWARE of what you buy cause you may or may not be able to return it for whatever reason...their 7 day return policy is pure marketing hype.

I am a heavy Lazada user. I am from the USA, but live in Thailand. The availability of many quality products is severely limited in the land of Smiles. Therefore, online shopping is an essential part of everyone's consumer activity. In the past Lazada was, albeit marginally, so-so. But after it was purchased by Ali Baba the quality has plummeted. No Domestic searching anymore (which forces disreputable Chinese merchants on everyone), absolutely NO customer service, the ability for merchants to decline honest refund requests (such as damaged, or wrong goods) and a litany of other problems. After 4 years and tens of thousands of dollars of spending on this site I have decided to close my account and go to other sites such as Shoppee. I think my action is illustrative of the general malaise most customers now feel towards Lazada.
